Mitch McConnell just revealed "skinny repeal" \-- a bill that from what we know about would cause 16 million people to lose health care -- and a vote is about to go down.

**Forget what you think you know, McConnell can get the 50 votes. Two GOP holdouts just came back on board.**

We need you to raise your voices TONIGHT. This is it. Obamacare is on the line.

[**Please say you 'll stand with Senate Democrats in TONIGHT's fight.**](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954da74/2b07114c/3495a99d/7312a2ee/1074995620/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/protect-our-care?source=PT-20170727-Breaking_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

name: The White House
subject: American Heroes Week
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2c9836f25f2b8e73acd05e83197d10c689ca84c92c6d4e5fe2e530b5f6f03ce3886ab19693210575482d31ad7594a498b6c846b5a42aaedd

THE PRESIDENT'S WEEKLY ADDRESS

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2c9836f25f2b8e73acd05e83197d10c689ca84c92c6d4e5fe2e530b5f6f03ce3886ab19693210575482d31ad7594a498b6c846b5a42aaedd

-------------

President Trump Recognizes Heroic First Responders
Yesterday, President Donald Trump awarded the Public Safety Medal of Valor to two Capitol Police Special Agents and three Alexandria Police Officers who exhibited extraordinary valor as the first responders, to the Alexandria, Virginia, baseball field shooting on June 14, 2017. The President expressed deep gratitude for the heroic efforts of the first responders as well as prayers for the victims.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2c9836f25f2b8e73da982bc77d9d9caea9c918acf9dba992a75fb93a0ccb7a0e67367f42c19214f1ec83e1dfa4fe19bd9ccf813b2ed4e6a6
Watch the event

Vice President Pence Swears in the U.S. Ambassador to Japan
Yesterday, Vice President Pence swore in the new U.S. Ambassador to Japan, Bill Hagerty.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2c9836f25f2b8e73e658f9b904b1d7b1ee169f6ff3c69ded41aad29720656e761650bf1ba3f13b21daadb5fb084d004f598cf24ebb92ed46
Watch the Ceremony

President Trump Donates Presidential Salary to Department of Education
This quarter, President Trump will be donating his salary to the Department of Education. Secretary of Education Betsy DeVos thanked the President, "The President is committed to our Nation's students and to reforming education in America so that every child, no matter their ZIP code, has access to a high-quality education."

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2c9836f25f2b8e73374856c12c9a881f67f200c8a21a0f18ee055a0b562a9376e4dd7ef1a509b7c1f131f0be7fa04a811ac10dd39d9c99ca
Read more

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump awards the Medal of Valor

| July 27, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS and VP TODAY

Today, President Trump will travel to New York and give remarks on MS-13 to Federal, State, and local law enforcement.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2c9836f25f2b8e730d40ec377ba34f58a5085219f3b4fc091c222455af64e28c312a867f5b30b144a6f4c4bfe8027a1077df8a53108cd947
Watch Live at 1:40pmEST

Coming Up This Week

On Sunday, Vice President Pence will travel abroad to Eastern Europe to advance President Trump's America First agenda and to strengthen our partnerships in the region.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2c9836f25f2b8e73ead2df76ff116e0272bc87d47083cf9d765f001fade142124210a0664471c06cc2ae667297b08c243aab4388526c796f
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=2c9836f25f2b8e7301dd4d063bf658b12eaa3f27f8c993c1603b58883042ddefc9d701b5b48dd3bf62cfb60477acbd5fe30137b8a8721d1b
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d43c1abc402c38f4312749c9959d6f9b4639eb9b5dbc633738f9f56f6b98cb6b15c3a273e50ff4664dee06c300237d3d2a
Unsubscribe

subject: Fake News CNN needs to hear this.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Republican National Committee
body:
They’ll be screaming the same lies... |
---
| | |

Peter,

Can you believe it? I've been in office now for six months, fighting back against the liberals and their crony agenda for YOU -- the Americans who have supported me through thick and thin.

DESPITE their endless lies and the flood of Fake News, we've accomplished so much already -- _all thanks to YOUR incredible support_. Together we've brought back thousands of jobs, fought back against radical Islamic terrorists, and scrapped the CORRUPT globalist deals that drain our wallets and weaken America.

But the Washington status quo _HATES_ what we're doing. They've made a lot of money off of business as usual. And now they're fighting back...

That's why I'm turning to YOU as our end-of-month deadline approaches, Peter. We must end the month STRONG.

name: Tom Perez
subject: I know we can always count on you, Derek
body:
From the bottom of my heart, thank you
---
Derek --

To all those who organized in your communities, who attended town hall meetings, who made calls or signed petitions, who came to Washington to make your voice heard -- thank you.

**There's no doubt in my mind that your tireless advocacy is what saved the Affordable Care Act. You made this possible, Derek.**

We'll have more fights ahead of us. Republicans will almost certainly make another attempt at repeal. They'll keep trying to undo President Obama's legacy and pass their agenda that benefits the wealthy at the expense of working people. We will need to keep organizing, and we will need to keep standing with the Democrats in Congress who are fighting for the values we hold most dear.

But for now, we can take comfort in having protected health care for millions of Americans -- and take pride in the power of this movement.

Thank you again, from the bottom of my heart, for being part of this fight. I know we'll always be able to count on you.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee

Friends,

The first step toward leveling the playing field of our democracy is drawing legislative and congressional districts that are fair.

Here in North Carolina, we know the need for fair maps. Last year, Democrats won nearly 50% of the vote, yet they hold only 38% of the seats in the State House and 30% of the seats in the State Senate.

We can't continue a system where our politicians pick their voters instead of the other way around.

**That's why we started Break the Majority. And that's why I need you now. Our goal right now is to raise $10,000 before the end of July. Can you help us get there?** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: It just went down in the Senate
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6488/24c69fd9/c0efb728/189961dc/4100187713/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE738N_digital_email_20170728_SenTcareVoteFail_inact&source=WQEFE738N_digital_email_20170728_SenTcareVoteFail_inact")
---

Peter,

Trumpcare is done (for now).

It just went down in the Senate -- and I am proud to report that every single EMILY's List senator voted against it.

The grassroots activism that mobilized to stop this bill has been beyond inspiring. And I'm so proud of how hard our women in the House and Senate stood firm against this terrible legislation that would have kicked upwards of ten million Americans off their insurance AND defunded Planned Parenthood.

We need more women like them in Congress -- because we shouldn't come this close to passing such dangerous, unpopular legislation ever again.
**Donate $3 now to help bring some common sense back to Congress and elect pro-choice Democratic women:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/No-More-Trumpcare](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6488/24c69fd9/c0efb728/189961dd/4100187713/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170727_email_SenTcareVoteFail?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgNoMoreTrumpcare&utm_campaign=WQEFE738N_digital_email_20170728_SenTcareVoteFail_inact&source=WQEFE738N_digital_email_20170728_SenTcareVoteFail_inact")**

Thanks,

body:
Friend --

Your House Republicans are working with President Trump to get your priorities done. We ran on your agenda -- the people's agenda -- and now we're executing on it.

Already, we've had the biggest increase in border security funding in a decade, we're improving and rebuilding the military, we've rolled back the Dodd Frank Law, and the House acted to pass our repeal and replace plan of Obamacare.

name: Tim Kaine
subject: YOU did this, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

Hundreds of thousands of calls, meetings, and rallies. Even some protests on Capitol Hill. YOU are the reason the Senate failed to repeal the Affordable Care Act last night. You -- along with millions of other Americans -- made your voices heard, loud and clear. Thank you.

The fight continues, Derek -- and it's more important than ever that we have folks in the Senate who will lead that fight, on health care and any number of other issues. **[Can I ask you to pitch in to help me stay in the Senate working for Virginians?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=LbNh9ekR5WNWNuRfKdMa9UWJt0JCWbL2)**

President Trump and his cohorts are moving on to their next strategy: building a 60-seat Republican majority in the Senate so no one will be able to stop their agenda.

Here's what's at stake: Last night's vote was 49-51. If we lose this seat, Republicans will have the votes to try to enact their radical agenda.

You've always had my back -- and Virginia's back. I need you to again now. **[Will you pitch in to ensure that I can continue representing you in the U.S. Senate?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=kDG5nScrtebfLr6eOOz2T0WJt0JCWbL2)**

Thank you,

Tim

President Trump and his cohorts are moving on to their next strategy: building a 60-seat Republican majority in the Senate so no one will be able to stop their agenda. Here's what's at stake: Last night's vote was 49-51. If we lose this seat, Republicans will have the votes to try to enact their radical agenda. You've always had my back -- and Virginia's back. I need you to again now. Thank you, Tim | | Paid for by Kaine for Virginia | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

subject: Time to work together on health care
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501130199%2501100256%2b-%2b07-28-2017%2beNewsletter%250114159.6322445.3471841.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501130199%2501100256%2b-%2b07-28-2017%2beNewsletter%250114159.6322445.3471841.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **July 28, 2018** |
---|---|---|---
| | |
#
Thanks to everyone who fought so hard to make sure the Senate did the right thing on health care! Millions of voices from across Maryland and around the country were heard in the Senate Chamber last night when the final vote was cast at 1:40 am. As a result of all the rallies, phone calls, town halls, and emails, we were able to stop Senate Republican efforts that would have resulted in huge price spikes and tens of millions of Americans losing access to affordable care - all to give tax breaks to the powerful few. While we are relieved, we must remain vigilant. These zombie WealthCare bills have sprung back to life many times over the last seven months. In addition, President Trump has openly revealed that he will use every tool at his disposal to actively sabotage the Affordable Care Act. I hope the Republican Senators and President Trump do not go down this road. Now is the time to roll up our sleeves and work together in a bipartisan way to improve health care for all Americans. Many of us have proposed common sense measures to immediately stabilize the Health Care Exchanges, as well as ideas to dramatically improve our overall health care system. The American people have spoken loud and clear. They do not want to reverse the progress we have made \-- they want to build on it in a way that makes affordable health care available to every American. Chris Van Hollen U.S. Senator ****
## Holding Russia, Iran, and North Korea Accountable
Congress has passed legislation to impose tough sanctions on Russia, Iran, and North Korea - with overwhelming bipartisan support - and we are urging President Trump to sign this package into law without delay. This bill imposes tough sanctions on Russia for its interference in our elections and prevents President Trump from removing those sanctions without the approval of the Congress. But our work is not done on North Korea sanctions - especially with the news today that Kim Jong-un has tested a second intercontinental ballistic missile this month. We must take immediate action to pass stronger sanctions that will squeeze North Korea's economy, sever its ties to the international financial system, and create the leverage necessary for successful nuclear negotiations. I've introduced bipartisan legislation with Senator Pat Toomey, called the Banking Restrictions Involving North Korea (BRINK) Act, to do just that. I look forward to working expeditiously with my colleagues to pass comprehensive sanctions on North Korea in the fall. ****
## A Better Deal for Marylanders
Too many Marylanders and folks across the country are working harder than ever but not getting ahead. Too many feel like they're on a treadmill going nowhere or falling further behind. They are being squeezed between flat or shrinking paychecks on the one hand, and rising costs on the other. And it is not enough to simply analyze or empathize with these challenges - we must have a plan to do something about it. That's why this week, I joined with Democrats in the House and Senate to lay out an economic plan that will give Americans 'A Better Deal.' The plan includes specific proposals to improve the lives of working families. We are going to promote good paying jobs. We are going to lower the cost of living for families, like the skyrocketing cost of prescription drugs and child care. And we are going to make sure that Americans have the skills, tools, and knowledge to get ahead. It is vital that we continue to fight President Trump's efforts to turn back the clock on progress in America. But it is not enough to only articulate what we are fighting against. We must also show what we are fighting for. <LINK to plan outline> ****
## Honoring Frederick Douglass
Frederick Douglass played a pivotal role in the history of Maryland and our nation, and next year marks the 200th anniversary of his birth. It is important for us to reflect upon his great achievements and pay tribute to his fight for freedom and justice. That's why I recently introduced the Frederick Douglass Bicentennial Commission Act. This legislation would establish a commission to honor and celebrate Frederick Douglass this bicentennial -- and will explore how we can celebrate his legacy and redouble our efforts to build an ever more perfect union. Enslaved at birth in Talbot County, he grew to become a national leader in the fight to end slavery and gain the right to vote for women. In his twenties, Douglass escaped slavery and eventually fled to England. In 1846, British Quakers raised funds to pay for his freedom and he returned home to the United States. He became one of the greatest orators, writers and social reformers in American history. His autobiography was a bestseller during his lifetime and Frederick Douglass played a central role in the abolitionist movement and became a world renowned leader in the fight to end the evil institution of slavery.

Millions of voices from across Maryland and around the country were heard in the Senate Chamber last night when the final vote was cast at 1:40 am. As a result of all the rallies, phone calls, town halls, and emails, we were able to stop Senate Republican efforts that would have resulted in huge price spikes and tens of millions of Americans losing access to affordable care - all to give tax breaks to the powerful few. While we are relieved, we must remain vigilant. These zombie WealthCare bills have sprung back to life many times over the last seven months. In addition, President Trump has openly revealed that he will use every tool at his disposal to actively sabotage the Affordable Care Act. I hope the Republican Senators and President Trump do not go down this road. Now is the time to roll up our sleeves and work together in a bipartisan way to improve health care for all Americans. Many of us have proposed common sense measures to immediately stabilize the Health Care Exchanges, as well as ideas to dramatically improve our overall health care system. The American people have spoken loud and clear. They do not want to reverse the progress we have made \-- they want to build on it in a way that makes affordable health care available to every American. Chris Van Hollen U.S. Senator ** Holding Russia, Iran, and North Korea Accountable Congress has passed legislation to impose tough sanctions on Russia, Iran, and North Korea - with overwhelming bipartisan support - and we are urging President Trump to sign this package into law without delay. This bill imposes tough sanctions on Russia for its interference in our elections and prevents President Trump from removing those sanctions without the approval of the Congress. But our work is not done on North Korea sanctions - especially with the news today that Kim Jong-un has tested a second intercontinental ballistic missile this month. We must take immediate action to pass stronger sanctions that will squeeze North Korea's economy, sever its ties to the international financial system, and create the leverage necessary for successful nuclear negotiations. I've introduced bipartisan legislation with Senator Pat Toomey, called the Banking Restrictions Involving North Korea (BRINK) Act, to do just that. I look forward to working expeditiously with my colleagues to pass comprehensive sanctions on North Korea in the fall. A Better Deal for Marylanders Too many Marylanders and folks across the country are working harder than ever but not getting ahead. Too many feel like they're on a treadmill going nowhere or falling further behind. They are being squeezed between flat or shrinking paychecks on the one hand, and rising costs on the other. And it is not enough to simply analyze or empathize with these challenges - we must have a plan to do something about it. That's why this week, I joined with Democrats in the House and Senate to lay out an economic plan that will give Americans 'A Better Deal.' The plan includes specific proposals to improve the lives of working families. We are going to promote good paying jobs. We are going to lower the cost of living for families, like the skyrocketing cost of prescription drugs and child care. And we are going to make sure that Americans have the skills, tools, and knowledge to get ahead. It is vital that we continue to fight President Trump's efforts to turn back the clock on progress in America. But it is not enough to only articulate what we are fighting against. We must also show what we are fighting for. <LINK to plan outline> ** Honoring Frederick Douglass Frederick Douglass played a pivotal role in the history of Maryland and our nation, and next year marks the 200th anniversary of his birth. It is important for us to reflect upon his great achievements and pay tribute to his fight for freedom and justice. That's why I recently introduced the Frederick Douglass Bicentennial Commission Act. This legislation would establish a commission to honor and celebrate Frederick Douglass this bicentennial -- and will explore how we can celebrate his legacy and redouble our efforts to build an ever more perfect union. Enslaved at birth in Talbot County, he grew to become a national leader in the fight to end slavery and gain the right to vote for women. In his twenties, Douglass escaped slavery and eventually fled to England. In 1846, British Quakers raised funds to pay for his freedom and he returned home to the United States. He became one of the greatest orators, writers and social reformers in American history. His autobiography was a bestseller during his lifetime and Frederick Douglass played a central role in the abolitionist movement and became a world renowned leader in the fight to end the evil institution of slavery. |

**_An Incredible Year_**
Wednesday marked one year since **Eric Holcomb** became our gubernatorial nominee, which was just six days after **Mike Pence** officially accepted our Party's nomination for vice president. Just think about the remarkable progress our state and nation have made during this incredible year of political twists and turns.

Thanks to President **Donald Trump** and Vice President Pence, we have a new mainstream conservative justice on the Supreme Court. We are cutting Obama-era regulations that were killing economic growth and we're working hard every day to Make America Great Again.

And here at home, Governor Holcomb continues his laser focus on the five pillars of his agenda. Just yesterday, in fact, he took another important step on the workforce development front by creating the position of Secretary of Career Connections and Talent. More about that below.
\- Kyle

(Photo: The VP and the Gov share a laugh at a Team Holcomb event in D.C., last week.)
---
|
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
Governor Holcomb this week released an [open letter to Hoosiers](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7bdab581fd&e=1acd9bb3d6) in which he outlined his stance on the federal health care debate. "We cannot continue to ignore the problems with our nation’s current health care system," Governor Holcomb wrote. "Medicaid and Medicare are shattering our federal budget, and the national debt has skyrocketed to $20 trillion. Obamacare is crumbling under its own weight. In Indiana, as many as 60 counties will be left with just one choice for insurance coverage under the Obamacare marketplace beginning in 2018. Premiums are rising, our Hoosier neighbors are losing access, and employers are frustrated by federal overregulation that makes it more difficult for Indiana to put Hoosiers to work." Read the full letter [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4e25fc78c5&e=1acd9bb3d6).

As mentioned above, Governor Holcomb also [announced this week that LaPorte Mayor Blair Milo will serve as Indiana's first-ever Secretary of Career Connections and Talent](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ca32ce8f1e&e=1acd9bb3d6). In making the announcement, Governor Holcomb said, "Our state’s unemployment rate has reached historically low levels. That means more Hoosiers are working, but it also presents talent challenges for our state’s employers, who need skilled and ready workers to keep their businesses thriving. This new position will provide the urgent and focused attention required to position Indiana well long-term and ensure our state’s workforce is keeping pace with our economy as it grows and becomes more diverse. I can think of no one better suited than Blair Milo to lead this charge.”

In this new role, Mayor Milo will "collaborate with business, industry, trade groups and public officials to identify employment needs, identify workers and help them take advantage of training opportunities for high-demand, high-wage positions. The secretary will work in partnership with Indiana colleges, universities, and certificate and training providers to connect Hoosiers to high-demand jobs," according to a [press release](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=614fd9a282&e=1acd9bb3d6) from the Governor's Office. You can read Chairman Hupfer's statement on the news [here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage2.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=69cd33a8d3&e=1acd9bb3d6).
|

| _**Volunteer at the Indiana State Fair**_
It's that time of year again! The Indiana State Fair kicks off on Friday, August 4th and the Indiana Republican Party will be there every single day from 10am-10pm at our booth located just north of the covered bridge and across from the Free Stage. We have three-hour long volunteer shifts open throughout the Fair if you're interested in helping staff the booth and spreading the good word about what Indiana Republicans are doing at the Statehouse and in D.C. Contact **James Cecil** at [jcecil@indiana.gop](mailto:jcecil@indiana.gop) to sign up for a shift or two or three!

| Friends, It's been hard to watch everything unfold in Washington this week. Republicans have been upending regular order and feverishly tried (and failed) to repeal the Affordable Care Act until the early hours of the morning. President Trump can't seem to stop Tweeting ridiculous, discriminatory policy announcements. Meanwhile, the American people are left wondering how these policies and actions will affect them. Will it mean less funding for their child's school? Will it mean cuts in assistance that a loved one relies on? [_**I intend to hold Trump and Republicans accountable, but I can 't do that alone. We have a big deadline coming up and missing it is not an option. Will you help out by contributing before you head out for the weekend?**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113ede/622b7b5b/4d1ce50/307bdf13/900639322/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse170728pmnd?refcode=dwse170728pmnd_m&recurring=true&amount=5") Republicans in Congress won't hold the President accountable. They have no interest in listening to the concerns of their constituents. That's why they have had no issue pushing forward with an incredibly cruel and dangerous health care bill. By hitting our goals, meeting our deadlines, and taking action, we can hold Trump and Republicans accountable. [_**Each dollar gets us closer to hitting this goal. body:
Help us hold Republicans accountable.
---
**Pitch in $3 or whatever you can to our Health Care Accountability Fund to take the fight to the Republicans who voted to take away our health care.

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052300f/4067b0a/3259d3cb9/7e6c4244/610012937/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Health-Care-Accountability-Fund-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170728_tp_nd&source=em_20170728_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170728_tp_nd")**

Derek, I'm not sure I've ever seen anything as dangerous or reckless in Washington as what Republicans tried to do this week with health care.

On Tuesday, fifty Senate Republicans and Mike Pence voted to move forward with repealing the Affordable Care Act despite not having any idea what the consequences would be. The Trump administration openly threatened senators for acting in the best interests of their constituents and opposing repeal. And the GOP flat-out ignored millions of calls, protests, and desperate pleas to save our health care.

**[Let's make something clear to Trump and his allies in Congress: we won't stop fighting to stop repeal, and we're going to make sure the Republicans who do vote to take our care away pay a hefty price.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052300f/4067b0a/3259d3cb9/7e6c4245/610012937/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/Health-Care-Accountability-Fund-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Lets+make+something+clear+to+Trump+and+h&utm_campaign=em_20170728_tp_nd&source=em_20170728_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170728_tp_nd")**

There are eight Republican senators up for re-election next year, including Dean Heller, who cast the deciding vote on Tuesday afternoon to move forward with repeal and then voted for repeal again early this morning. We will vote them out -- it's as simple as that.

Let's channel our frustrations and anger into electing Democrats all over the country. Before our deadline on Monday, pitch in $3 or whatever you can to our new Health Care Accountability Fund.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

[ QUICK DONATE: $3](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052300f/4067b0a/3259d3cb9/7e6c4242/610012937/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/help-democrats-fight-back-match?express_lane=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+QUICK+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20170728_tp_nd&source=em_20170728_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170728_tp_nd")

[QUICK DONATE: $50](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052300f/4067b0a/3259d3cb9/7e6c4241/610012937/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/help-democrats-fight-back-match?express_lane=true&amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+QUICK+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20170728_tp_nd&source=em_20170728_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170728_tp_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $100](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052300f/4067b0a/3259d3cb9/7e6c424e/610012937/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/help-democrats-fight-back-match?express_lane=true&amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+QUICK+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20170728_tp_nd&source=em_20170728_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170728_tp_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052300f/4067b0a/3259d3cb9/7e6c424f/610012937/VEsO/ "https://my.democrats.org/Health-Care-Accountability-Fund-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20170728_tp_nd&source=em_20170728_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170728_tp_nd")

Tom
